identity.name: Konjiki Ramen (Downtown)
identity.address: 41 Elm St, Toronto, ON M5G 1H1, Canada
identity.location: 41 Elm St, Toronto, ON M5G 1H1, Canada

narrative.summary: Ramen-focused Toronto spot with strong local following and dependable bowls.

blocks.title: The Space
blocks.body: You're on Elm Street in the downtown core, steps from Yonge-Dundas Square's chaos but worlds away once you step inside. The room is compact and functional—a counter where you can watch steam rise from pots, a handful of tables where regulars lean in close over their bowls. It's bright, efficient, the kind of place where the focus is squarely on what's in front of you, not the décor.

blocks.title: The Food
blocks.body: This is tonkotsu-style ramen done with care: creamy pork-based broths, thin handmade noodles, and toppings that actually matter. The original shoyu is clean and dependable, while the Smokey Black Tonkotsu leans into burnt garlic chilli oil for depth without overwhelming heat. Order the mini chashu if you want extra pork belly, or go for the deep-fried king mushrooms if you need something crisp to break up all that richness. Bowls run around $16-20, with the option to upsize if you're genuinely hungry.

blocks.title: Go When
blocks.body: This is your midweek solo dinner at the counter, or a quick post-work meal when you want something warming and satisfying without ceremony. Skip it on a rushed lunch—you want time to finish the broth.

blocks.title: The Vibe
blocks.body:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Very tasty ramen and appetisers. We ordered original shoyu & Spicy. The spicy ramen is not very spicy but very flavourful with burnt garlic chilly oil. The broth is creamy and tasty. Noodles are thin hand made!
We also orders mini chashu and Deep Fried King Mushrooms both were delicious.
Service is excellent and staff is friendly.
They have vegetarian options available. They have option to substitute the Pork chashu with Chicken but the broth is pork or seafood based. We ordered regular size and it’s perfect for a meal. You can also order super size if you want."

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Got a chance to try this ramen place. Friendly staff, and the food was really good. I had a bowl of Smokey Black Tonkotsu Ramen and a side of truffle gyoza. The gyoza’s didn’t come in time so they didn’t charge me for them! I appreciated that!  I will be back for sure!"

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "This place is definitely worth a visit for ramen lovers. I had the wagyu ramen and it was absolutely delicious. The flavors were so rich and satisfying. The service was also quite good, adding to the overall pleasant experience. I would highly recommend trying this dish. Of all the places I've visited for Ramen, this ranks the best as far as tastes goes."

blocks.title: The Menu
blocks.body:
FROZEN RAMEN KITS
• Ramen Kit A — $30.00
   3 ramen noodles and 3 soups with Tonkotsu varieties and choice of frozen toppings
• Ramen Kit B — $30.00
   3 ramen noodles and 3 soups featuring Spicy Miso and Tonkotsu varieties
⭐ Premium Kit — $36.00
   3 ramen noodles and 3 soups with Clam and Wagyu & Angus Beef varieties

RAMEN
⭐ Premium Wagyu Ramen — $38.00

• Signature Clam Intense Shoyu Ramen — $18.50

• Avocado Vegetarian Ramen — $18.50

APPETIZERS

• Chicken Karaage — $8.40
   5pc marinated deep fried chicken with spicy mustard sauce
• Lobster Gyoza — $10.30
• Gyoza — $7.40
   6pcs
• Edamame — $5.20
   Steam boiled salted edamame. Vegetarian

DONBURI
• Katsudon — $18.50
   Pork cutlet with Japanese egg sauce and steamed rice
• Mini Beef Gyu-Don — $18.00
• Oyako Don — $16.50
   Chicken & egg with soy sauce and rice
• Mini Pork Belly Don — $11.50
   Rice topped with braised pork belly, pickled ginger and scallions
• Mini Chashu Don — $10.00
   Rice topped with sous-vide pork chashu, pickled takana, and green scallions
